title: Review: core crate security/correctness round 11
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — Core crate logic is well-structured. The three findings are all in the security-adjacent zeroization class (two) and a usability/reliability class (one). No memory safety issues, no protocol logic bugs, no correctness issues found beyond those already tracked.
Confidence: 10% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) openmls internals — whether StaticSecret::from() internally zeroizes its input is crate-dependent and not verified; (2) x25519_dalek::StaticSecret zeroize-on-drop behavior not audited here. Would raise confidence if: cross-checked x25519_dalek StaticSecret::from() source to confirm whether it internally copies or moves without clearing.
